sentences.chinese: 子孝王景嗣，二十四年薨。
sentences.literal: The son, Prince Xiao Jing, succeeded; in twenty-four years he died.
sentences.idiomatic: His son Liu Jing, Prince Xiao of Chengyang, reigned twenty-four years.

sentences.chinese: 子哀王雲嗣，一年薨，無子，國絕。
sentences.literal: The son, Prince Ai Yun, succeeded; in one year he died; he had no sons; the state was cut off.
sentences.idiomatic: Liu Yun, Prince Ai, lasted only a year and left no heir; the kingdom lapsed.

sentences.chinese: 成帝復立雲兄俚為城陽王，王莽時絕。
sentences.literal: Emperor Cheng again established Yun's elder brother Li as king of Chengyang; in Wang Mang's time it was cut off.
sentences.idiomatic: Emperor Cheng revived the title for Liu Li, Prince Yun's elder brother, but the line died out under Wang Mang.

sentences.chinese: 濟北王興居初以東牟侯與大臣共立文帝於代邸，曰：「誅呂氏，臣無功，請與太僕滕公俱入清宮。」
sentences.literal: King Xingju of Jibei, at first as Marquis of Dongmou together with the great ministers jointly established Emperor Wen at the Dai residence, saying: "In executing the Lü, your servant had no merit; I beg to enter with Grand Coachman Lord of Teng together to purge the palace."
sentences.idiomatic: Liu Xingju of Jibei had been Marquis of Dongmou when he helped the ministers enthrone Emperor Wen at the princely residence in Dai. "We slew the Lü," he said then, "yet I had no share in the glory. Let me join Grand Coachman Xiahou Ying in clearing the palace."

sentences.chinese: 遂將少帝出，迎皇帝入宮。
sentences.literal: Thereupon he led the young emperor out and welcomed the emperor into the palace.
sentences.idiomatic: He escorted the boy emperor out and brought the new sovereign in.

sentences.chinese: 立十一年，孝景三年，吳楚反，膠東、膠西、菑川、濟南王皆發兵應吳楚。
sentences.literal: Having been established eleven years, in the third year of Xiaojing, Wu and Chu rebelled; the kings of Jiaodong, Jiaoxi, Zichuan, and Jinan all raised troops in response to Wu and Chu.
sentences.idiomatic: Eleven years into his reign came the third year of Emperor Jing, when Wu and Chu rose in revolt. The kings of Jiaodong, Jiaoxi, Zichuan, and Jinan all mobilized in their support.

sentences.chinese: 欲與齊，齊孝王狐疑，城守不聽。
sentences.literal: They wished to join with Qi; Prince Xiao of Qi hesitated, defended the city, and would not heed.
sentences.idiomatic: They tried to bring Qi in, but Prince Xiao of Qi wavered, kept his walls manned, and refused their summons.

sentences.chinese: 三國兵共圍齊，齊王使路中大夫告於天子。
sentences.literal: The troops of the three states together besieged Qi; the king of Qi sent the road middle grandee to report to the Son of Heaven.
sentences.idiomatic: The three rebel hosts ringed Qi, so the king dispatched his courier—the official styled "grandee in the middle of the road"—to appeal to the emperor.

sentences.chinese: 天子復令路中大夫還報，告齊王堅守，漢兵今破吳楚矣。
sentences.literal: The Son of Heaven again ordered the road middle grandee to return and report, telling the king of Qi to hold firm; Han troops had now broken Wu and Chu.
sentences.idiomatic: The court sent the same envoy back with orders for the king to stand fast: imperial forces had already crushed Wu and Chu.

sentences.chinese: 路中大夫至，三國兵圍臨菑數重，無從入。
sentences.literal: When the road middle grandee arrived, the troops of the three states besieged Linzi in many rings; there was no way to enter.
sentences.idiomatic: But Linzi was wrapped in layer upon layer of siege lines, and he could not get through.

sentences.chinese: 三國將與路中大夫盟曰：「若反言漢已破矣，齊趣下三國，不且見屠。」
sentences.literal: The generals of the three states swore an oath with the road middle grandee, saying: "If you reverse your words that Han is already defeated, Qi will hurry to submit to the three states; if not, you will presently see slaughter."
sentences.idiomatic: The rebel generals cornered him and extorted a pledge: "Tell the king the Han is finished and urge him to yield to us—or we kill you."

sentences.chinese: 路中大夫既許，至城下，望見齊王，曰：「漢已發兵百萬，使太尉亞夫擊破吳楚，方引兵救齊，齊必堅守無下！」
sentences.literal: The road middle grandee having promised, when he reached below the walls and saw the king of Qi, he said: "Han has already raised a million troops, sent Grand Commandant Zhou Yafu to break Wu and Chu, and is now leading troops to rescue Qi; Qi must hold firm and not submit!"
sentences.idiomatic: He gave his word, yet when he stood beneath the ramparts in sight of the king he shouted that a million Han soldiers were on the march under Grand Commandant Zhou Yafu, that Wu and Chu were already broken, and that Qi must not capitulate.

sentences.chinese: 三國將誅路中大夫。
sentences.literal: The generals of the three states executed the road middle grandee.
sentences.idiomatic: The three generals put him to death for his treachery.

sentences.chinese: 齊初圍急，陰與三國通謀，約未定，會路中大夫從漢來，其大臣乃復勸王無下三國。
sentences.literal: At first, when Qi's siege was urgent, secretly it communicated with the three states in plotting; the agreement was not yet settled, when it happened that the road middle grandee came from Han; its great ministers then again urged the king not to submit to the three states.
sentences.idiomatic: Early in the siege Qi had secretly dealt with the rebels, though no firm pact was sealed. When the courier returned from the capital with the emperor's message, Qi's ministers pressed the king anew to hold out against the three kingdoms.

sentences.chinese: 會漢將欒布、平陽侯等兵至齊，擊破三國兵，解圍。
sentences.literal: It happened that Han generals Luan Bu, Marquis of Pingyang, and others' troops reached Qi, attacked and broke the troops of the three states, and lifted the siege.
sentences.idiomatic: Soon Luan Bo and the Marquis of Pingyang arrived with other imperial columns, routed the besiegers, and raised the siege.

sentences.chinese: 已後聞齊初與三國有謀，將欲移兵伐齊。
sentences.literal: Afterward, hearing that at first Qi had plotted with the three states, they wished to shift troops to attack Qi.
sentences.idiomatic: Afterward the court learned of Qi's first overtures to the rebels and prepared to turn the army against Linzi.

sentences.chinese: 齊孝王懼，飲藥自殺。
sentences.literal: Prince Xiao of Qi feared, drank poison, and killed himself.
sentences.idiomatic: Prince Xiao of Qi, terrified at the prospect, took his own life with poison.

sentences.chinese: 而膠東、膠西、濟南、菑川王皆伏誅，國除。
sentences.literal: And the kings of Jiaodong, Jiaoxi, Jinan, and Zichuan were all executed; their states were abolished.
sentences.idiomatic: The kings of Jiaodong, Jiaoxi, Jinan, and Zichuan were executed to a man and their kingdoms struck from the rolls.

sentences.chinese: 獨濟北王在。
sentences.literal: Only the king of Jibei remained.
sentences.idiomatic: Only the king of Jibei survived among them.

sentences.chinese: 其母曰紀太后。
sentences.literal: His mother was called Empress Dowager Ji.
sentences.idiomatic: His mother was known as Empress Dowager Ji.

sentences.chinese: 太后取其弟紀氏女為王后，王不愛。
sentences.literal: The empress dowager took her younger brother Ji's clan daughter as queen for the king; the king did not love her.
sentences.idiomatic: She married him to a niece of her own Ji clan, but the king cared nothing for his Ji queen.

sentences.chinese: 紀太后欲其家重寵，令其長女紀翁主入王宮正其後宮無令得近王，欲令愛紀氏女。
sentences.literal: Empress Dowager Ji wished her family to have heavy favor; she ordered her eldest daughter, Princess Ji, to enter the king's palace and regulate his rear palace, not allowing anyone to get near the king; she wished to make him love the Ji clan daughter.
sentences.idiomatic: Empress Dowager Ji meant to keep power in her kin: she installed her eldest daughter, a princess by the Ji surname, inside the palace to police the harem and keep other women from the king's bed, hoping to force his affection onto the Ji girl.

sentences.chinese: 王因與其姊翁主姦。
sentences.literal: The king therefore committed adultery with his elder sister the princess.
sentences.idiomatic: Instead the king took his sister, that same princess, as his secret lover.

sentences.chinese: 齊有宦者徐甲，入事漢皇太后。
sentences.literal: Qi had a eunuch Xu Jia, who entered service with the Han empress dowager.
sentences.idiomatic: Among Qi's eunuchs was Xu Jia, who had gone to Chang'an to wait on the empress dowager.

sentences.chinese: 皇太后有愛女曰脩成君，脩成君非劉氏子，太后憐之。
sentences.literal: The empress dowager had a beloved daughter called Princess Xiucheng; Princess Xiucheng was not a child of the Liu house; the empress dowager pitied her.
sentences.idiomatic: The empress dowager doted on a daughter named the Princess of Xiucheng—no child of the imperial Liu line—and showered her with tenderness.

sentences.chinese: 脩成君有女娥，太后欲嫁之於諸侯。
sentences.literal: Princess Xiucheng had a daughter E; the empress dowager wished to marry her to one of the feudal lords.
sentences.idiomatic: The princess had a daughter, E, whom her mother hoped to marry off to a feudal king.

sentences.chinese: 宦者甲乃請使齊，必令王上書請娥。
sentences.literal: The eunuch Jia then requested to be sent to Qi, insisting he would make the king submit a memorial asking for E.
sentences.idiomatic: Xu Jia volunteered for a mission to Qi, promising to bring back a formal request from the king for the girl's hand.

sentences.chinese: 皇太后大喜，使甲之齊。
sentences.literal: The empress dowager was greatly pleased and sent Jia to Qi.
sentences.idiomatic: Delighted, she dispatched him north.

sentences.chinese: 時主父偃知甲之使齊以取后事，亦因謂甲：「即事成，幸言偃女願得充王後宮。」
sentences.literal: At that time Zhufu Yan knew Jia was being sent to Qi to take a queen affair, and therefore also said to Jia: "If the affair succeeds, fortunately speak that Yan's daughter wishes to be able to fill the king's rear palace."
sentences.idiomatic: Zhufu Yan, seeing a chance for his own daughter, pulled Xu Jia aside: "If you pull this off, put in a word for my girl as a secondary consort."

sentences.chinese: 甲至齊，風以此事。
sentences.literal: When Jia reached Qi, he hinted at this matter.
sentences.idiomatic: In Linzi, Xu Jia sounded out the court on the match.

sentences.chinese: 紀太后怒曰：「王有后，後宮備具。
sentences.literal: Empress Dowager Ji angrily said: "The king has a queen; the rear palace is fully provided.
sentences.idiomatic: Empress Dowager Ji exploded: "The king already has a queen and a full harem.

sentences.chinese: 且甲，齊貧人，及為宦者入事漢，初無補益，乃欲亂吾王家！
sentences.literal: Moreover Jia is a poor man of Qi who, having become a eunuch and entered service with Han, at first had no benefit, and now wishes to throw our royal house into disorder!
sentences.idiomatic: As for Xu Jia—a pauper from Qi who bought his way into the Han palace—he has never done us a scrap of good, and now he would sow chaos in our house!

sentences.chinese: 且主父偃何為者？
sentences.literal: Moreover what is Zhufu Yan?
sentences.idiomatic: And who is this Zhufu Yan?

sentences.chinese: 乃欲以女充後宮！」
sentences.literal: He wishes to use his daughter to fill the rear palace!"
sentences.idiomatic: He thinks his daughter belongs in the inner apartments!"

sentences.chinese: 甲大窮，還報皇太后曰：「王已願尚娥，然事有所害，恐如燕王。」
sentences.literal: Jia was greatly cornered, returned and reported to the empress dowager, saying: "The king was already willing to marry E, yet the affair had something harmful, and he feared it would be like the king of Yan."
sentences.idiomatic: Xu Jia, trapped, reported to the empress dowager that the king had seemed willing to take E until scruples arose—scruples like those that had undone the king of Yan.

sentences.chinese: 燕王者，與其子昆弟姦，坐死。
sentences.literal: The king of Yan, with his son and younger brothers, committed adultery and sat liable to death.
sentences.idiomatic: He meant the scandal of the king of Yan, who had lain with his kindred and paid with his life.

sentences.chinese: 故以燕感太后。
sentences.literal: Therefore by Yan he moved the empress dowager.
sentences.idiomatic: The allusion was calculated to freeze the empress dowager's matchmaking.

sentences.chinese: 太后曰：「毋復言嫁女齊事。」
sentences.literal: The empress dowager said: "Speak no more of marrying a daughter to Qi."
sentences.idiomatic: "Say no more of wedding a girl to Qi," she said.

sentences.chinese: 事寖淫聞於上。
sentences.literal: The affair gradually seeped and was heard by the sovereign.
sentences.idiomatic: Whispers of the incest spread until they reached the emperor's ear.

sentences.chinese: 主父偃由此與齊有隙。
sentences.literal: Zhufu Yan thereby had a rift with Qi.
sentences.idiomatic: From that day Zhufu Yan nursed a grudge against Qi.

sentences.chinese: 偃方幸用事，因言：「齊臨菑十萬戶，巿租千金，人眾殷富，鉅於長安，非天子親弟愛子不得王此。
sentences.literal: Yan was then favored and wielded power, and therefore said: "Qi's Linzi has a hundred thousand households; market rents are a thousand in gold; the people are numerous, rich, and greater than Chang'an; unless the Son of Heaven's own younger brother or beloved son, none may be king there.
sentences.idiomatic: Now in favor at court, he observed that Linzi boasted a hundred thousand households, market tolls counted in pounds of gold, and wealth rivaling the capital itself—ground fit only for the emperor's closest kin.

sentences.chinese: 今齊王於親屬益疏。」
sentences.literal: Now the king of Qi is ever more distant in kinship."
sentences.idiomatic: Yet the present king of Qi was only a remote cousin.

sentences.chinese: 乃從容言呂太后時齊欲反，及吳楚時孝王幾為亂。
sentences.literal: Then at ease he spoke of how in Empress Dowager Lü's time Qi had wished to rebel, and how in the time of Wu and Chu Prince Xiao had almost made disorder.
sentences.idiomatic: He reminded the throne how Qi had flirted with rebellion under Empress Lü and how Prince Xiao had nearly joined the Seven Kingdoms revolt.

sentences.chinese: 今聞齊王與其姊亂。
sentences.literal: Now he heard the king of Qi had committed adultery with his elder sister.
sentences.idiomatic: He added the fresh rumor that the king was sleeping with his own sister.

sentences.chinese: 於是武帝拜偃為齊相，且正其事。
sentences.literal: Thereupon Emperor Wu appointed Yan chancellor of Qi, and moreover to rectify the affair.
sentences.idiomatic: The emperor named Zhufu Yan chancellor of Qi with orders to get to the bottom of it.

sentences.chinese: 偃至齊，急治王後宮宦者為王通於姊翁主所者，辭及王。
sentences.literal: When Yan reached Qi, he urgently prosecuted the rear-palace eunuchs who for the king had communicated with the elder sister princess's place; their confessions implicated the king.
sentences.idiomatic: Zhufu Yan reached Linzi and turned the screws on the harem eunuchs who had facilitated the king's visits to the princess; their testimony pointed straight at the king.

sentences.chinese: 王年少，懼以罪為吏所執誅，乃飲藥自殺。
sentences.literal: The king was young in years; fearing he would be seized and executed by officials for his crime, then he drank poison and killed himself.
sentences.idiomatic: Young and terrified of arrest and public execution, the king drank poison.

sentences.chinese: 是時趙王懼主父偃壹出敗齊，恐其漸疏骨肉，乃上書言偃受金及輕重之短，天子亦因囚偃。
sentences.literal: At this time the king of Zhao feared that Zhufu Yan in one outing had ruined Qi, and feared he would gradually estrange flesh and bone; therefore he submitted a memorial saying Yan had taken bribes and had faults of severity and lightness; the Son of Heaven therefore also imprisoned Yan.
sentences.idiomatic: The king of Zhao, alarmed that one vindictive minister could destroy a princely house and set kin against kin, memorialized that Zhufu Yan had taken bribes and committed other offenses; the emperor had him thrown in prison.

sentences.chinese: 公孫弘曰：「齊王以憂死，無後，非誅偃無以塞天下之望。」
sentences.literal: Gongsun Hong said: "The king of Qi died of grief, without posterity; unless Yan is executed there is no way to stop the world's expectations."
sentences.idiomatic: Gongsun Hong urged: "The king of Qi died heirless and broken-hearted; only Zhufu Yan's death will satisfy the realm."

sentences.chinese: 偃遂坐誅。
sentences.literal: Yan thereupon sat guilty and was executed.
sentences.idiomatic: Zhufu Yan was condemned and put to death.

sentences.chinese: 濟北王
sentences.literal: King of Jibei
sentences.idiomatic: The king of Jibei

sentences.chinese: 濟北王志，吳楚反時初亦與通謀，後堅守不發兵，故得不誅，徙王菑川。
sentences.literal: King Zhi of Jibei, at the time of the Wu and Chu rebellion, at first also communicated in plotting with them; afterward he held firm and did not raise troops; therefore he was not executed, and was moved to be king of Zichuan.
sentences.idiomatic: Liu Zhi of Jibei had toyed with the rebels at first but never marched; spared execution, he was transferred to the throne of Zichuan.

sentences.chinese: 元朔中，齊國絕。
sentences.literal: In the Yuanshuo period, the state of Qi was cut off.
sentences.idiomatic: During Yuanshuo the kingdom of Qi ceased to exist.

sentences.chinese: 悼惠王後唯有二國：城陽、菑川。
sentences.literal: After Prince Daohui there were only two states: Chengyang and Zichuan.
sentences.idiomatic: Of Prince Daohui's line only Chengyang and Zichuan remained.

sentences.chinese: 菑川地比齊，武帝為悼惠王冢園在齊，乃割臨菑東圜悼惠王冢園邑盡以予菑川，令奉祭祀。
sentences.literal: Zichuan bordered Qi; Emperor Wu, because Prince Daohui's tomb park was in Qi, then cut off east of Linzi the round tomb park of Prince Daohui and its towns, entirely giving them to Zichuan, ordering it to perform the sacrifices.
sentences.idiomatic: Because Prince Daohui's mausoleum park lay east of Linzi inside old Qi territory, Emperor Wu carved that enclave out and annexed it to Zichuan so his descendant could tend the sacrifices.

sentences.chinese: 志立三十五年薨，是為懿王。
sentences.literal: Zhi reigned thirty-five years and died; this is Prince Yi.
sentences.idiomatic: Liu Zhi reigned thirty-five years and is remembered posthumously as Prince Yi of Zichuan.

sentences.chinese: 子靖王建嗣，二十年薨。
sentences.literal: The son, Prince Jing Jian, succeeded; in twenty years he died.
sentences.idiomatic: His son Liu Jian, Prince Jing, ruled twenty years.

sentences.chinese: 子頃王遺嗣，三十五年薨。
sentences.literal: The son, Prince Qing Yi, succeeded; in thirty-five years he died.
sentences.idiomatic: Liu Yi, Prince Qing, held the throne thirty-five years.

sentences.chinese: 子思王終古嗣。
sentences.literal: The son, Prince Si Zhonggu, succeeded.
sentences.idiomatic: Liu Zhonggu, Prince Si, succeeded him.

sentences.chinese: 五鳳中，青州刺史奏終古使所愛奴與八子及諸御婢姦，終古或參與被席，或白晝使羸伏，犬馬交接，終古親臨觀。
sentences.literal: In the Wufeng period, the Qingzhou governor memorialized that Zhonggu made his beloved male slaves fornicate with his bazhong (Eight Sons) concubines and various palace maids; Zhonggu sometimes joined them on the couch, or in broad daylight made the weak lie prone while dogs and horses copulated, Zhonggu personally watching.
sentences.idiomatic: During Wufeng the governor of Qingzhou charged Liu Zhonggu with forcing his favorite male slaves on concubines of the statutory bazhong rank—the grade called 'Eight Sons'—and on other palace women, taking part himself, and—in broad daylight—making frail attendants submit to bestial coupling while he watched.

sentences.chinese: 產子，輒曰：「亂不可知，使去其子。」
sentences.literal: When children were born, then he said: "Disorder cannot be known; make them cast away their children."
sentences.idiomatic: When children resulted, he ordered them destroyed: "We cannot tell whose seed this is—get rid of it."

sentences.chinese: 事下丞相御史，奏終古位諸侯王，以令置八子，秩比六百石，所以廣嗣重祖也。
sentences.literal: The affair went down to the chancellor and imperial clerk; they memorialized that Zhonggu, occupying the position of a feudal king, by ordinance had installed bazhong (Eight Sons) concubines, salary comparable to six hundred shi, by which to broaden heirs and honor the ancestors.
sentences.idiomatic: The case went to the chancellor and the censorate, who observed that the statutes allow a king to take bazhong concubines at six hundred bushels' stipend precisely to multiply heirs and honor his line.

sentences.chinese: 而終古禽獸行，亂君臣夫婦之別，悖逆人倫，請逮捕。
sentences.literal: Yet Zhonggu behaved like birds and beasts, disordering the distinction between lord and minister and husband and wife, perverse and contrary to human relations; they requested arrest.
sentences.idiomatic: Yet Zhonggu had conducted himself like a beast, blurring every bond of ruler and subject, husband and wife, and human decency; they demanded his arrest.

sentences.chinese: 有詔削四縣。
sentences.literal: There was an edict stripping four counties.
sentences.idiomatic: An edict stripped him of four counties.

sentences.chinese: 二十八年薨。
sentences.literal: In twenty-eight years he died.
sentences.idiomatic: He died in the twenty-eighth year of his reign.

sentences.chinese: 子考王尚嗣，五年薨。
sentences.literal: The son, Prince Kao Shang, succeeded; in five years he died.
sentences.idiomatic: His son Liu Shang, Prince Kao, reigned five years.

sentences.chinese: 子孝王橫嗣，三十一年薨。
sentences.literal: The son, Prince Xiao Heng, succeeded; in thirty-one years he died.
sentences.idiomatic: Liu Heng, Prince Xiao, ruled thirty-one years.

sentences.chinese: 子懷王交嗣，六年薨。
sentences.literal: The son, Prince Huai Jiao, succeeded; in six years he died.
sentences.idiomatic: Liu Jiao, Prince Huai, died after six years.

sentences.chinese: 子永嗣，王莽時絕。
sentences.literal: The son Yong succeeded; in Wang Mang's time it was cut off.
sentences.idiomatic: His son Liu Yong inherited the title until Wang Mang extinguished the house.

sentences.chinese: 贊曰：悼惠之王齊，最為大國。
sentences.literal: The appraisal says: When Prince Daohui was king of Qi, it was the greatest state.
sentences.idiomatic: The historian's judgment: Of all the great fiefs, Prince Daohui's Qi was the mightiest.

sentences.chinese: 以海內初定，子弟少，激秦孤立亡藩輔，故大封同姓，以填天下。
sentences.literal: Because within the seas had just been settled, sons and younger kin were few, stimulated by Qin's isolation and lack of screen and aid, therefore they greatly enfeoffed those of the same surname, in order to fill the realm.
sentences.idiomatic: The empire was newly pacified, imperial sons were few, and the lesson of Qin's lonely fall was fresh; the court therefore lavished territory on Liu kinsmen to ring the realm with bulwarks.

sentences.chinese: 時諸侯得自除御史大夫群卿以下眾官，如漢朝，漢獨為置丞相。
sentences.literal: At that time the feudal lords could themselves appoint from the imperial clerk and great ministers down the multitude of officials, as in the Han court; Han alone for them established the chancellor.
sentences.idiomatic: Each kingdom could staff its own bureaucracy down to the clerks, mirroring the capital, save that the crown alone named each kingdom's chancellor.

sentences.chinese: 自吳楚誅後，稍奪諸侯權，左官附益阿黨之法設。
sentences.literal: From after Wu and Chu were executed, gradually they stripped the feudal lords' power; the laws of left-hand officials, attachment, faction, and partisan bias were established.
sentences.idiomatic: After Wu and Chu were crushed, the throne steadily pared princely power—through statutes against serving princes as a "left-hand" career, against unauthorized enlargement of fiefs, and against clique loyalty.

sentences.chinese: 其後諸侯唯得衣食租稅，貧者或乘牛車。
sentences.literal: Thereafter the feudal lords only obtained to clothe and eat from rents and taxes; the poor among them sometimes rode ox carts.
sentences.idiomatic: In time the kings kept little more than the income from their tax rolls; the poorer among them went about in ox-drawn carts.
